Title: Search Bar - Click Opens Dropdown Empty State
Preconditions: Storefront open at homepage; search input has no text
Steps:
1. Locate the search input labeled 'Search' in main navigation
2. Click the search input
Expected Result:
- Dropdown opens below the input
- 'Hints' section visible with 1+ prior search terms as clickable buttons
- Each hint shows a search icon and query text
- 'Start typing to search' text visible
- 'Check all products' link visible pointing to /catalog
- No product results shown yet
- CONSOLE: No JavaScript errors
- NETWORK: No failed API calls

Title: Search Bar - Autocomplete Shows Product Previews While Typing
Preconditions: Storefront open; products matching 'hoodie' exist in catalog
Steps:
1. Click the search input
2. Type 'hoodie' one character at a time
3. Wait for dropdown update after each character
4. Observe final dropdown with all 6 characters typed
Expected Result:
- 'Products' section appears in dropdown
- Each entry: thumbnail image + product name (clickable link) + price
- At least 4 products shown (Vintage Colorado Hoodie $54.00; Hoodie Base product with only File req From $250.00; Hoodie Base with Only File non required $300.00; Black California Beach Pullover Hoodie From $19.00)
- 'Hints' section remains visible above Products
- Clear (X) button appears in input
- NETWORK: GraphQL searchProducts query fires with keyword: 'hoodie'
- CONSOLE: No JS errors

Title: Search Bar - Hints Section Shows Prior Searches on Focus
Preconditions: At least 2 prior searches exist ('laptop' and 'hoodie' are visible as hints)
Steps:
1. Click the search input
2. Observe the 'Hints' section label
3. Observe hint items (icon + text)
4. Click the hint button labeled 'laptop'
Expected Result:
- Section labeled exactly 'Hints' (not 'Recent searches')
- Each hint is a button with search icon and query text
- Clicking 'laptop' populates input with 'laptop'
- Browser navigates to /search?q=laptop
- Search results for 'laptop' display

Title: Search Bar - Clear Button Resets Input to Empty
Preconditions: Storefront open at any page
Steps:
1. Click the search input
2. Type 'hoodie'
3. Verify clear (X) button appears
4. Click the clear (X) button
Expected Result:
- After typing: clear button visible; input contains 'hoodie'
- After click: input is empty
- Dropdown returns to 'Hints' + 'Start typing to search' state
- Clear button disappears
- User stays on same page (no navigation)

Title: Search Bar - Clicking Autocomplete Product Navigates to PDP
Preconditions: Storefront open at homepage; 'Vintage Colorado Hoodie' in catalog
Steps:
1. Click the search input
2. Type 'hoodie'
3. Wait for autocomplete product results
4. Click 'Vintage Colorado Hoodie' in the dropdown
Expected Result:
- Browser navigates to /products-with-options/configurable-caps-shirts/hoodie
- PDP loads with product name 'Vintage Colorado Hoodie'
- Search dropdown closes
- No JavaScript errors

Title: Search Bar - Check All Products Link Goes to Catalog
Preconditions: Storefront open at homepage
Steps:
1. Click the search input (do not type)
2. Verify 'Check all products' link is visible
3. Click 'Check all products'
Expected Result:
- Browser navigates to /catalog
- Full product catalog page loads
- Dropdown closes

Title: Search - Submit via Enter Key
Preconditions: Storefront open at any page
Steps:
1. Click the search input
2. Type 'hoodie'
3. Press the Enter key
Expected Result:
- Browser navigates to /search?q=hoodie
- Results page loads
- Heading: 'Your search for hoodie returned the following 4 results' (count may vary)
- 'hoodie' is bold/emphasized in heading
- Search input retains 'hoodie'
- Products displayed in grid view by default

Title: Search - Submit via Search Button (Magnifier Icon)
Preconditions: Storefront open at any page
Steps:
1. Click the search input
2. Type 'hoodie'
3. Click the 'Search' button (magnifier icon)
Expected Result:
- Browser navigates to /search?q=hoodie
- Results page loads identically to SRCH-NEW-007
- Same products and count as Enter key submission

Title: Search Results - Result Count in Heading Is Accurate
Preconditions: Navigate to /search?q=hoodie; current count is 4 products
Steps:
1. Navigate to https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com/search?q=hoodie
2. Read the page heading
3. Count the product cards in the grid
Expected Result:
- h1 reads: 'Your search for hoodie returned the following 4 results'
- Count in heading (4) matches actual number of product cards rendered

Title: Search Results - Grid and List View Toggle
Preconditions: Navigate to /search?q=hoodie (4 results)
Steps:
1. Navigate to /search?q=hoodie
2. Confirm default grid layout (multiple product columns)
3. Click 'Switch to list view'
4. Verify list layout (single-column with more detail)
5. Click 'Switch to grid view'
6. Verify grid layout returns
Expected Result:
- Default is grid view
- List view: single-column format
- Grid view: multi-column layout
- Product count consistent (4) across all view switches
- No JS errors during switching
- Active view button has visual active state

Title: Search Results - Sort Options Change Product Order
Preconditions: Navigate to /search?q=hoodie (multiple results with varying prices)
Steps:
1. Navigate to /search?q=hoodie
2. Note available sort options in 'Sort by:' dropdown (default: 'Featured')
3. Select 'Price: Low to High'
4. Record product order by price
5. Select 'Price: High to Low'
6. Verify order reversed from step 4
7. Select 'Name: A to Z' or equivalent
8. Verify alphabetical order
9. Select 'Featured' to reset
Expected Result:
- Sort dropdown has at least Featured and price sort options
- 'Price: Low to High': ascending price order ($19 before $54 before $250)
- 'Price: High to Low': descending price order
- Alphabetical sort: products in A-Z order by name
- Product count stays at 4 after each sort change
- URL updates to reflect selected sort

Title: Search Results - Show in Stock Checkbox Default and Toggle
Preconditions: Navigate to /search?q=hoodie
Steps:
1. Navigate to /search?q=hoodie
2. Verify 'Show in stock' checkbox is checked by default
3. Verify 'Show in stock' chip is in the chip bar
4. Uncheck 'Show in stock'
5. Observe chip bar and result count
6. Re-check 'Show in stock'
7. Verify chip returns
Expected Result:
- 'Show in stock' is checked on page load
- Chip labeled 'Show in stock' is present in chip bar
- Unchecking removes chip; result count may change
- Re-checking restores chip and filtered results
- 'Reset filters' button appears/disappears based on active non-default filters

Title: Search Results - Purchased Before Checkbox
Preconditions: User authenticated with prior purchases matching 'hoodie'; navigate to /search?q=hoodie
Steps:
1. Verify 'Purchased before' is unchecked by default
2. Check 'Purchased before'
3. Verify 'Purchased before' chip appears
4. Verify results filter to prior purchases only
5. Uncheck 'Purchased before'
6. Verify chip disappears and full results return
Expected Result:
- 'Purchased before' defaults to unchecked
- Checking adds a chip and filters to prior purchase items
- Unchecking removes chip and restores all results

Title: Search Results - Sidebar Facet Filter
Preconditions: Navigate to /search?q=hoodie; sidebar shows Color facet
Steps:
1. Click 'Color' facet group header to expand
2. Select a color value (e.g., 'Malachite')
3. Observe chip bar and result count
4. Verify chip labeled with selected color appears
5. Verify result count updates
6. Click 'Close chip' on the color chip
7. Verify full results return
Expected Result:
- Sidebar facet groups are expandable
- Selecting a value creates a chip in the active-filter bar
- Product list updates to matching items only
- 'Close chip' removes filter and restores full count
- No errors during facet apply/remove

Title: Search Results - Reset Filters Preserves Search Term
Preconditions: Navigate to /search?q=hoodie; apply at least one sidebar facet filter
Steps:
1. Navigate to /search?q=hoodie
2. Apply a sidebar facet filter (e.g., Color: Malachite)
3. Verify 'Reset filters' button is visible
4. Click 'Reset filters'
Expected Result:
- All applied filters cleared (beyond any defaults)
- Search term 'hoodie' remains in the header search input
- URL retains ?q=hoodie; filter params are removed
- Full result set for 'hoodie' restored
- 'Reset filters' button disappears after all non-default filters cleared

Title: Search - No Results State UI Validation
Preconditions: Storefront is accessible
Steps: 1. Navigate to https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com/search?q=xyzqwerty9876543
Expected Result:
- h1: Sorry, your search for "xyzqwerty9876543" didn't return any results
- Search term is bold/emphasized in heading
- 'There are no results found' message visible
- 'Reset search' button visible
- No product cards displayed
- No sidebar filter panel shown
- No 'Did you mean...?' suggestion
- URL is /search?q=xyzqwerty9876543

Title: Search - Reset Search Button Clears Query
Preconditions: Navigate to /search?q=xyzqwerty9876543 (no-results page is showing)
Steps:
1. Verify no-results state and 'Reset search' button are visible
2. Click 'Reset search'
Expected Result:
- Search input in header is cleared
- User is redirected from the no-results page (to homepage or catalog)
- No JavaScript error occurs

Title: Search - Whitespace-Only Query Handling
Preconditions: Storefront open at any page
Steps:
1. Click the search input
2. Type '   ' (3 spaces only)
3. Press Enter or click the Search button
Expected Result:
- Either: no navigation occurs (submission blocked for whitespace-only input)
- Or: if navigation occurs, user lands on no-results or catalog page
- Application does not crash or throw a JS error
- NETWORK: No 5xx response

Title: Search - Very Long Query (200+ Characters)
Preconditions: Storefront open at any page
Steps:
1. Click the search input
2. Type a 200-character string of repeated 'a' characters
3. Press Enter
Expected Result:
- Input accepts the long string (or enforces visible max-length constraint)
- If submitted: no-results page loads without errors
- No JS error, no 500 error, no application crash
- URL is properly URL-encoded

Title: Search - XSS Script Injection in Input
Preconditions: Storefront is accessible
Steps:
1. Click the search input
2. Type: <script>alert('xss')</script>
3. Press Enter
4. Observe browser for alert dialog
5. Read results page heading
6. Check browser console for script execution
Expected Result:
- No JavaScript alert dialog appears
- Script is not executed
- URL encodes the input (e.g., %3Cscript%3E...)
- Results page heading displays query as literal text
- CONSOLE: No alerts, no XSS-related errors

Title: Search - SQL Injection Attempt
Preconditions: Storefront is accessible
Steps:
1. Click the search input
2. Type: ' OR '1'='1
3. Press Enter
Expected Result:
- No database error, exception page, or stack trace displayed
- Results page handles query gracefully
- Application remains stable
- NETWORK: API response is 200
- CONSOLE: No error-level messages

Title: Search - Special Characters Are URL-Encoded Correctly
Preconditions: Storefront open at any page
Steps:
1. Search for 'hoodie & jeans' -- check URL
2. Search for 'product (new)' -- check URL
3. Search for 'item #123' -- check URL
Expected Result:
- 'hoodie & jeans': URL encodes ampersand (%26)
- 'product (new)': URL encodes parentheses (%28, %29)
- 'item #123': URL encodes hash (%23)
- All searches: results page loads without error
- h1 displays decoded (human-readable) query text
- NETWORK: All requests return 200

Title: Search - Direct URL Navigation with Query Parameter
Preconditions: Storefront is accessible
Steps:
1. Open a new browser tab
2. Navigate directly to: https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com/search?q=hoodie
Expected Result:
- Search results page loads correctly (no prior homepage visit needed)
- Header search input pre-populated with 'hoodie'
- Heading shows correct result count for 'hoodie'
- Products matching 'hoodie' are displayed
- Sidebar filters and sort dropdown are functional

Title: Search - Empty Query Parameter Loads Catalog
Preconditions: Storefront is accessible
Steps: 1. Navigate directly to: https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com/search?q=
Expected Result:
- Page loads without error (no 404, no 500)
- Page title is 'QA & Catalog' (catalog-style page)
- Products are displayed (not a no-results state)
- Search input is empty or contains no query

Title: Search Bar - Keyboard-Only Navigation
Preconditions: Storefront open at homepage; keyboard-only operation
Steps:
1. Press Tab repeatedly from top of page until search input is focused
2. Verify visible focus indicator on input
3. Type 'hoodie' via keyboard
4. Verify autocomplete dropdown appears
5. Press Tab or Arrow Down to move focus into dropdown
6. Use Arrow keys to navigate hint items
7. Press Enter on a hint item to execute search
8. Repeat steps 1-3 then press Escape to close dropdown
Expected Result:
- Search input reachable via Tab
- Visible focus indicator present when focused
- Typing triggers autocomplete dropdown
- Arrow keys navigate within dropdown
- Enter on hint item executes search
- Escape closes dropdown and returns focus to input
- No keyboard trap
- CONSOLE: No keyboard-related JS errors

Title: Search Bar - ARIA Roles and Labels
Preconditions: Storefront open at homepage; DevTools Accessibility panel available
Steps:
1. Inspect search input role and accessible name (DevTools Accessibility)
2. Inspect 'Barcode scan' button accessible name
3. Inspect 'Search' button accessible name
4. Type in input then inspect clear (X) button accessible name
5. With dropdown open inspect dropdown container role and aria attributes
Expected Result:
- Search input: role='searchbox'; accessible name='Search'
- 'Barcode scan': accessible name is 'Barcode scan'
- 'Search' button: accessible name is 'Search'
- Clear button: non-empty accessible name describing its purpose
- Dropdown: role='listbox' or equivalent ARIA pattern
- All dropdown interactive elements have non-empty accessible names

Title: Search - Autocomplete Response Time Under 2 Seconds
Preconditions: Storefront open at homepage; DevTools Network tab open
Steps:
1. Open DevTools Network tab
2. Click search input and type 'hoodie'
3. Start timer from first character typed
4. Observe when autocomplete product results appear
5. Note GraphQL request duration in Network tab
Expected Result:
- Autocomplete product results appear within 2 seconds of first character
- GraphQL API call completes under 1500ms
- No loading spinner visible for more than 2 seconds

Title: Search - Results Page Load Time Under 3 Seconds
Preconditions: DevTools Network tab open; throttling set to 'No throttling'
Steps:
1. Navigate to /search?q=hoodie from a fresh navigation
2. Measure time from navigation start until product cards and heading are visible and interactive
Expected Result:
- Results page fully rendered within 3 seconds
- h1 heading with result count visible within 2 seconds
- No major layout shift after initial product render
- NETWORK: All calls return 200; no 4xx or 5xx

2. Verify search input visible in header

Title: Search - Mobile Results Page Layout 375px
Preconditions: Chrome DevTools device emulation at 375px; navigate to /search?q=hoodie
Steps:
1. Set viewport to 375px
2. Navigate to /search?q=hoodie
3. Verify results heading readable
4. Verify product cards use 1 or 2 column grid
5. Verify sort dropdown accessible (no horizontal scroll)
6. Verify filter controls accessible (e.g., collapsed behind 'Filters' button)

Expected Result:

Title: Search - GA4 search Event Fires on Query Submission
Preconditions: DevTools Console open; Google Tag Manager configured on storefront
Steps:
1. Open DevTools Console
2. Run window.dataLayer to confirm array exists
3. Click search input; type 'hoodie'; press Enter
4. After results load run: window.dataLayer.filter(e => e.event === 'search')
Expected Result:
- window.dataLayer is an array and exists
- A search event found: { event: 'search', search_term: 'hoodie' }
- search_term matches submitted query exactly
- CONSOLE: No GA4 or GTM errors

Title: Search - GA4 view_search_results Event on Results Page
Preconditions: DevTools Console open
Steps:
1. Navigate to /search?q=hoodie
2. In Console run: window.dataLayer.filter(e => e.event === 'view_search_results')
Expected Result:
- At least one view_search_results event in dataLayer
- Event includes search_term: 'hoodie'
- CONSOLE: No GA4 or GTM errors

Title: Search to Cart - Full End-to-End Flow
Preconditions: User authenticated; cart is empty or at known state
Steps:
1. Click search input; type 'hoodie'; press Enter
2. Verify navigation to /search?q=hoodie
3. Locate 'Vintage Colorado Hoodie' ($54.00) on results page
4. Click '+' (Increase quantity) on the product card to set quantity 1
5. Verify success notification or cart icon badge increments
6. Click 'Cart' link in navigation
7. Verify 'Vintage Colorado Hoodie' is in the cart at $54.00
Expected Result:
- Search finds product on results page
- Quantity stepper functional on search result cards
- Cart badge increments by 1
- Cart page shows: name 'Vintage Colorado Hoodie'; price $54.00; quantity 1
- NETWORK: GraphQL addItem mutation returns success
- CONSOLE: No errors during entire flow

Title: Search - Browser Back Returns to Results Page
Preconditions: User navigated from search results to a product PDP
Steps:
1. Navigate to /search?q=hoodie
2. Click 'Vintage Colorado Hoodie' to open PDP
3. Verify PDP loads
4. Click browser Back button
Expected Result:
- Browser returns to /search?q=hoodie
- Search results page displayed with same query and results
- Header search input still shows 'hoodie'
- Product cards visible (page not blank or in error state)
